summary:
The throughline across these two days is a shift from feature-building to building the machinery that governs feature-building. Session Repack, MarkItDown, and scheduler fixes are real but secondary now — the standards system (catalog + check-standards skill + pre-commit hook, now joined by a smart-quotes hook) is the thing actually compounding. It moved from "stood up" on 6/22 to "enforced and feeding back into itself" on 6/23: rework and bugs are starting to get codified as new standards rather than just patched, which is the self-improving loop you were aiming for.

Two things are converging in parallel and may collide soon: Agent Composer is being redesigned as a "trigger-to-output, convention-enforcing registry" — that's the same instinct as the standards system, applied to agents instead of code. Backlog grooming is also moving from manual to agent-driven review. All three (standards enforcement, agent composition, backlog grooming) are reaching toward one idea: Claude maintaining the rules and pipeline that govern its own work, not just doing the work. Worth naming that convergence explicitly before building further, rather than evolving three separate registries that end up needing to be reconciled later.

Momentum: standards/governance work is accelerating (two days running, now with enforcement teeth). Session Repack is steady incremental progress (edit/range/skip controls). MarkItDown and scheduler bug triage look done/stable — no new mentions of friction there on 6/23. The interrupt/resume-heavy build pattern noted on 6/23 suggests these sessions are exploratory/converging-on-design rather than execution-mode — consistent with your stated preference to settle the abstraction before code lands.
